---
title: "Yeast_Project"
author: "Krish"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## **Data Acquisition**

```{r}
# 1. Create the data directory
dir.create("data/raw_reads", recursive = TRUE)

# 2. Define the download URL
yeast_url <- "ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/ERR153/001/ERR1539001/ERR1539001.fastq.gz"

# 3. Download the file
# method="wget" is used for robustness with larger sequencing files
download.file(url = yeast_url, 
              destfile = "data/raw_reads/ERR1539001.fastq.gz", 
              method = "wget")

# 4. Verify the file is present
list.files("data/raw_reads/")
```

## **The Audit & Filter (Replication)**

### Sequali report(Pre-filtered)

```{Bash}
#| eval: fale
# Activate your environment
conda activate sequali_env

# Run Sequali
# --outdir: specifies where to save the HTML report
mkdir reports
sequali --outdir reports/sequali_pre data/raw_reads/ERR1539001.fastq.gz

# Check the directory you are in, if you are in data/raw_reads already use this:
# sequali --outdir reports/sequali_pre ./ont.reads.fastq.gz
```

### Sequali report(Post-filtered)

```{R}
dir.create("data/clean_reads")
```

```{bash}
conda install filtlong

filtlong --min_length 1100 --keep_percent 90 data/raw_reads/ERR1539001.fastq.gz | gzip > data/clean_reads/ERR1539001_Clean.fastq.gz
 
 
 sequali --outdir reports/sequali_post data/clean_reads/ERR1539001_Clean.fastq.gz
```
